investors: ITI Growth Opportunities Fund | Merisis Wealth Trust | PROMAFT Partners | V3 Ventures
summary: The Hosteller raised Rs 150 crore in a Series B round co-led by PROMAFT Partners and V3 Ventures, with participation from ITI Growth Opportunities Fund and Merisis Wealth Trust, to fund expansion of its hostel network across India.

investors: InfoEdge Ventures | Kae Capital | Susquehanna Venture Capital
summary: GobbleCube, a Gurugram-based AI startup for digital commerce, raised $15 million in a Series A round led by Susquehanna Venture Capital, with participation from InfoEdge Ventures and Kae Capital.
Digital lending platform KreditBee raised $280 million in a Series E round, led by Motilal Oswal Alternates, achieving unicorn status with a $1.5 billion post‑money valuation.

investors: AdvantEdge Founders | Asian Development Bank | IvyCap Ventures | Trucks Venture Capital
summary: Astranova Mobility raised Rs 60 crore in a Series A round to expand its EV financing and asset management platform, with IvyCap Ventures leading the round.

Pluckk, a digital-first fresh food and clean-label FMCG brand, raised Rs 100 crore in a Series A round led by Euro Gulf Investment to expand offline retail, enter international markets, and enhance its AI-driven supply chain.
Atlas, a Singapore-based startup, raised $6 million in a seed round co‑led by Accel and Stellaris to expand its AI platform for independent accounting firms in North America.
Palmonas, a demi-fine jewellery startup, raised $40 million in a Series B round.
Servify raised $7.8 million in an ongoing Series D round in March 2025, led by BEENEXT, after previously raising $65 million Series D led by Singularity Growth Opportunities Fund.
IndiGo Ventures Fund invested about Rs 10 crore in air-taxi startup Sarla Aviation, which will use the funds for operations and expansion.

summary: Men's personal care brand Unbound raised Rs 8 crore in a funding round led by Fireside Ventures to launch its first product range across skin, hair, and body care.

SILA, a Mumbai-based real‑estate business services platform, raised $100 million from private equity firm Permira to fund expansion and technology upgrades across India.

The article reports Bijak's FY25 financial performance, noting a 25% drop in GMV to Rs 551 crore and increased losses of Rs 61 crore, without announcing a new funding round.
